codeReview.summary: The reviewed feature satisfies VAL-SAVE-001. The committed regression adds a dedicated Store model and a targeted integration test that resets the embedded DB inside the test via ensure_db()->reinit_db(test_db_path()), performs the first save without any manual DEFINE TABLE setup, and verifies a subsequent get() returns the same row. The broader commit also changes explicit-id foreign persistence paths in core/src/lib.rs, but those edits do not invalidate the fresh-db proof and no blocking defects were found within the assigned feature scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill still frames fresh-db features as requiring a new failing regression and implementation work, but this worker completed correctly by verifying an already-present regression/implementation. The skill should explicitly allow completion-by-verification when the assigned feature is already implemented before the worker starts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges requests this clarification, and the transcript skeleton shows no code edits were needed before the worker ended successfully.

summary: Reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit for milestone-a-fresh-db-first-save-proof. The feature passes review: the test fresh_db_save_requires_no_manual_define_table proves first save on a freshly reinitialized embedded DB succeeds without manual DEFINE TABLE and get() still works afterward.
